item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 7)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: The Sagrada Família: "Like a dream come true"
item.description: Soaring 50 stories above Barcelona, the Sagrada Família basilica has been under construction for nearly a century and a half – the improbable dream of architect Antoni Gaudí, who died 100 years ago, leaving behind clues to complete his masterpiece.

item.title: The Sagrada Família
item.description: The Sagrada Família basilica, soaring 50 stories above Barcelona, has been under construction for nearly a century and a half. It was the improbable dream of architect Antoni Gaudí, who died in 1926 with less than 15 percent of the structure complete. Correspondent Seth Doane talks with architects following the clues Gaudí left behind to complete his masterpiece.

item.title: The films of Steven Spielberg
item.description: A look at the career of one of Hollywood's most celebrated filmmakers

item.title: "Disclosure Day" director Steven Spielberg on alien visitations
item.description: As a child, Steven Spielberg stared at a meteor shower and began his love affair with the sky. The director of the 1977 classic "Close Encounters of the Third Kind" returns with "Disclosure Day," which imagines closely-held secrets surrounding alien visitations.

item.title: An exhibit fit for a queen: The fashion of Elizabeth II
item.description: During her lifetime, and her 70-year reign as Britain's monarch, Queen Elizabeth II's wardrobe was as important diplomatically as any speech she gave. A new exhibition on view at Buckingham Palace in London, "Queen Elizabeth II: Her Life in Style," is the most comprehensive look at her wardrobe, covering every decade of her life. Correspondent Alina Cho pays a visit, and also talks with fashion designer Erdem Moralioglu about how the Queen inspired his work.

item.title: Extended interview: Steven Spielberg
item.description: In this web exclusive, director Steven Spielberg talks with Turner Classic Movies host Ben Mankiewicz about his latest film, "Disclosure Day," and the science fiction influences on his work. He also discusses his beliefs about alien civilizations, given his depictions of extra-terrestrial life in some of his most popular movies.

item.title: Steven Spielberg on "Disclosure Day" and alien visitations
item.description: As a child, Steven Spielberg stared at a meteor shower on a wondrous starry night and began his love affair with the sky. The director of the classic "Close Encounters of the Third Kind" and "E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial" has returned to the sci-fi genre with "Disclosure Day," which imagines closely-held secrets surrounding alien visitations. He talks with Turner Classic Movies host Ben Mankiewicz about UAP/UFO phenomena, the paranormal, and his own beliefs regarding intelligent life beyond Earth.

item.title: The promises and pitfalls of GLP-1 drugs
item.description: Approved 20 years ago as a diabetes treatment, GLP-1 drugs have been found to help patients reduce weight, changing the lives of more than 30 million people in the U.S. But there also have been troubling side effects reported.

item.title: Passage: In memoriam
item.description: "Sunday Morning" remembers some of the notable figures who left us this week, including soul singer Peabo Bryson.

item.title: These United States: Broadway's George M. Cohan
item.description: Mo Rocca celebrates the career of a founding father of American theater: George M. Cohan (1878-1942), an actor, playwright, producer, and composer of such standards as "Give My Regards to Broadway," "The Yankee Doodle Boy," and "Over There."

item.title: "Ragtime": A timeless musical
item.description: The Broadway revival of the musical (nominated for 11 Tony Awards) depicts drama, joy and heartbreak in the pursuit of the American Dream at the turn of the 20th century, with parallels to the contemporary world, from issues of race to the immigrant experience.

item.title: Meet Marina Marchese, the honey sommelier
item.description: Marina Marchese is America's first honey sommelier – an expert trained in identifying countless yet subtle differences in honey. She talks with correspondent Serena Altschul about the buzz surrounding her specialized knowledge of all things honey, and what consumers should beware when seeking unadulterated honey.

item.title: Young applicants discuss the challenges of today's job search
item.description: With the unemployment rate for young workers about twice as high as the national average, "Sunday Morning" talks with recent graduates from across the country about how AI is affecting both their prospects and the hiring process itself.

item.title: Help wanted: The difficulties facing job applicants
item.description: The unemployment rate for young workers is about twice as high as the national average. With young workers seeking entry-level positions being thwarted by a crushing job market, correspondent David Pogue talks with recent graduates from across the country about how AI is affecting both their prospects and the hiring process itself. He also talks with experts about how to adjust job searches, and about fields that are hungry for new workers.

item.title: The promises and pitfalls of GLP-1 drugs
item.description: Approved 20 years ago as a treatment for diabetes, GLP-1 drugs have been found also to help patients significantly reduce weight. More than 30 million people in the U.S. have had their lives changed by GLP-1 medications. But there have also been troubling side effects reported. Correspondent Tracy Smith talks with experts who say the drugs might prove useful in treating other diseases associated with obesity (including cancer); and with patients who have taken GLP-1 drugs and experienced widely varying reactions.

item.title: 1 dead after Palestinian man with Israeli citizenship opens fire in Israel
item.description: The number of places where people were shot initially raised concerns that there could be multiple, coordinated attackers.

item.title: Almanac: June 7
item.description: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

item.title: Extended interview: The cast of "Ragtime"
item.description: In this web exclusive, Broadway stars Brandon Uranowitz, Joshua Henry and Caissie Levy (each a Tony Award-nominated star of the musical revival "Ragtime") talk with correspondent Mo Rocca about their emotional connections with the show's story and themes – as artists, audience members, and cast members from past productions.

item.title: "Ragtime" and the pursuit of the American Dream
item.description: As depicted in the Broadway revival of the musical "Ragtime" (nominated for 11 Tony Awards), the pursuit of the American Dream was just as dramatic, expansive and heartfelt at the turn of the 20th century as it is today. Correspondent Mo Rocca talks with Tony-nominated actors Joshua Henry, Caissie Levy and Brandon Uranowitz, and with lyricist Lynn Ahrens and composer Stephen Flaherty, about the show's parallels to the contemporary world, from issues of race to the immigrant experience.

item.title: 5 taken to the hospital after strong winds at NCAA tournament
item.description: Video from the storm showed rain and wind that reached speeds of 40 mph tearing up a tent, with one person flying through the air while trying to hold it down as another person rolls uncontrollably down a hill.

item.title: Steven Spielberg watches scene from "Close Encounters of the Third Kind"
item.description: In this web exclusive, Steven Spielberg, director of the new film "Disclosure Day," walks Turner Classic Movies host Ben Mankiewicz through a scene from his 1977 UFO classic "Close Encounters of the Third Kind," discussing Richard Dreyfuss' performance, the widescreen aspect ratio, and John Williams' music.

item.title: Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire as stalemate continues in peace talks
item.description: The U.S. military reported that it has shot down six Iranian one-way attack drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz, while seven ballistic missiles fired at Kuwait and Bahrain were largely intercepted as well.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: After woman's murder, detectives learn killer was "only half the story"
item.description: After Alyssa Burkett was murdered in broad daylight in Carrollton, Texas, Andrew Beard, the father of her child, became a suspect. Investigators would eventually discover a twisted murder plot they say was orchestrated by his fiancée, Holly Elkins.

item.title: Texas mom's killer received text before murder: "I hope you handle it"
item.description: After Alyssa Burkett was murdered, detectives quickly learned that the prime suspect was the father of her child, Andrew Beard. But as the investigation unfolded, they would find out that Beard wasn't the only one involved in the murder plot.

item.title: The Plot to Eliminate Alyssa Burkett
item.description: A toxic couple orchestrates an elaborate plan to kill a mother. "48 Hours" correspondent Peter Van Sant reports.

item.title: 6/6: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Dangerous storms threaten multiple states; Iran accuses U.S. of violating the ceasefire as both sides exchange strikes.

item.title: No bags, no watch parties: What to expect when Trump attends NBA Finals
item.description: As President Trump prepares to watch the New York Knicks take on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden, officials are planning for a heightened security posture, sources said.

item.title: Colombia's Petro accuses Trump of intervening in his country's election
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro argues the U.S. has chosen to align against his government and back forces he identifies as complicit in the drug trade.
